topic: Area of Study 4: Popular Music and Jazz
subject: music

items.why: Students confuse andante (walking, moderate) with adagio (slow). Andante literally means 'going/walking' in Italian - it's a comfortable pace, not slow.

items.q: How does D Dorian differ from D minor?
items.correct: Dorian has a raised 6th (B natural) compared to natural minor.
items.random_wrong: Dorian has no sharps or flats at all.
items.why: Students treat modes as variants of major/minor. Dorian on D uses only white keys (like C major, but starting on D), giving a raised 6th compared to D natural minor - producing the modal flavour.

items.q: How many semitones are in a perfect fifth?
items.why: Students count fifth as 'five steps' but the name refers to letter NAMES (1-2-3-4-5), not semitones. C to G spans 5 letter names but 7 semitones (C-C#-D-D#-E-F-F#-G).

items.q: In what city was Motown Records founded in 1959?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Detroit. “New York” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which band's 1977 album 'Never Mind the Bollocks' is a landmark of British punk?
items.why: Correct answer: The Sex Pistols. “The Clash” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year was Motown Records founded by Berry Gordy?
items.why: Exact answer: 1959. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
